steps.title: Locate the angle
steps.content:
The angle is:
$$\frac{5\pi}{4}=225^\circ$$
This is in the third quadrant.

steps.title: Use the reference angle
steps.content:
The reference angle is:
$$\frac{5\pi}{4}-\pi=\frac{\pi}{4}$$
The cosine of the reference angle is:
$$\cos(\frac{\pi}{4})=\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$$

steps.title: Apply the quadrant sign
steps.content:
Cosine is negative in the third quadrant, so:
$$\cos(\frac{5\pi}{4})=-\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$$
The answer is A.
